title: ptisana squamosa
intro: Ptisana squamosa, also known as the rough horsetail or Australian horsetail, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties due to its high silica content, which is said to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Ptisana squamosa include its potential to improve skin health, boost the immune system, and support bone health. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
